text.title: Solo KPI Dashboard Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for a solo-SaaS KPI dashboard at $0–50k ARR. Optimised for review-fatigue prevention, not analytical depth.

rule.statement: The dashboard has exactly six tiles, in this order: weekly signups, activation rate (signed up → did core action within 7 days), MRR, gross monthly churn %, open support tickets, weekly active accounts. No seventh tile is added without removing one.
rule.rationale: Solo operators abandon dashboards above ~7 metrics within 4 weeks. A fixed roster forces prioritisation and survives review fatigue.

rule.statement: Each tile is sourced from either (a) a single SQL query against the app DB, or (b) a single REST/GraphQL call to Stripe / Plausible / support tool. No ETL, no warehouse, no transformations beyond the query itself.
rule.rationale: ETL pipelines at this scale cost more in maintenance than the insight they yield. Direct queries break loudly when the schema changes — preferable to silently stale dashboards.

rule.statement: Dashboard review is a fixed 30-minute Friday slot. The operator writes one note per tile: "expected / unexpected / action". If the slot runs long, cut analysis depth, not the slot.
rule.rationale: Variable-length review meetings get skipped. A 30-minute time-box makes the ritual durable.

rule.statement: Each metric has exactly one definition stored next to the dashboard (Notion page, README, sheet tab). Conflicting definitions in marketing slides, investor updates, or internal docs are rejected; everything cites the dashboard definition.
rule.rationale: Metric-definition drift is the #1 source of "the numbers don't match" arguments — fatal for one person trying to make decisions.

rule.statement: Forbidden tiles: page views without conversion, total signups (cumulative), Twitter followers, GitHub stars, total users ever. If a metric cannot drive a decision the operator would take on Friday, it is removed.
rule.rationale: Vanity metrics consume review attention and bias decisions toward audience-growth theatre over revenue motion.

example.code: -- Tile: activation rate (signed up in last 30d → did core action within 7d) SELECT ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN first_core_action_at - created_at <= INTERVAL '7 days' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 1) AS activation_pct FROM users WHERE created_at >= now() - INTERVAL '30 days';
example.note: One query, one tile, one number. No joins to a warehouse fact table.

antipattern.description: Operator builds a 14-tile Metabase board "to have it ready when the team grows" and stops opening it after week 3.
antipattern.reason: Violates r1 (tile count) and r3 (no fixed review slot). Tool sophistication scales ahead of operational need.
